content.body: The tree_construction tests suggest that they should be processed using the rules for 'in body', at least they pass when doing that.
The tree_construction part is resolved thanks to https://github.com/web-platform-tests/wpt/pull/61085. Only the tokenizer tests remain broken
I opened a PR for this: https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12653
Shannon Booth: curious if Attr in ranges came up in Ladybird as apparently there was no real test coverage. I created some over at https://github.com/web-platform-tests/wpt/pull/61121
content.formatted_body: Shannon Booth: curious if Attr in ranges came up in Ladybird as apparently there was no real test coverage. I created some over at https://github.com/web-platform-tests/wpt/pull/61121

content.body: I'm not 100% sure what you mean, but we pass those tests too
But StaticRange indeed throws per spec
Shannon Booth: just wondering if it was problematic that there was no test coverage. I guess maybe not. It does seem like the spec is adequate.
content.formatted_body: Shannon Booth: just wondering if it was problematic that there was no test coverage. I guess maybe not. It does seem like the spec is adequate.

content.body: Ah! Yeah I think we just got the behaviour for free due to inheritance
